name: Homes in Partnership, INC.
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit housing counselor and affordable home builder serving low-to-moderate income families across Central Florida with free homeownership education and energy-efficient housing.
description_long:
Homes In Partnership, Inc. is a non-profit housing counseling agency and affordable housing provider operating across five Central Florida counties: Citrus, Orange, Lake, Marion, and Sumter. The organization was founded on the principle that safe, affordable homeownership should be accessible to families at all income levels, particularly those earning very-low to moderate incomes who face barriers to traditional home financing.

The company offers three primary service lines: HUD-approved housing counseling programs that provide comprehensive homeownership education and guidance from application through move-in; the Homes by HIP program, which develops and sells fair-market affordable homes built to modern energy efficiency standards; and a Mutual Self-Help building program that allows qualified families to build their own homes. All housing counseling services are provided at no cost to consumers, consistent with HUD-approved non-profit standards.

Homes In Partnership differentiates itself through its integrated approach combining education with tangible housing solutions. Rather than counseling alone, the organization removes barriers by actually building energy-efficient homes designed for long-term affordability and sustainability. Their focus on reducing energy costs through efficient construction and their explicit service to very-low-income households distinguishes them from standard for-profit builders and mainstream lenders.

This organization is genuinely well-suited for families seeking free, expert guidance on homeownership without sales pressure or profit motives. The primary limitation is geographic—services are restricted to five specific Florida counties—and availability may be subject to funding and program capacity. Prospective homebuyers should verify current program eligibility and availability before engaging.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling program ensures compliance with federal standards and genuine non-profit credentials
Completely free housing counseling services from application through move-in—no hidden fees or charges
Builds energy-efficient homes designed to reduce long-term utility costs for low-income families
Serves very-low-income households often excluded by traditional lenders and builders
Offers three distinct pathways to homeownership: counseling, direct purchase, or mutual self-help building
Operates across five counties providing geographic reach within Central Florida
Comprehensive counseling covers the entire homeownership journey, not just initial qualification
cons: Service area limited to five specific Florida counties (Citrus, Orange, Lake, Marion, Sumter)—not available nationwide or in most states
Website lacks specific details about program eligibility requirements, wait times, or current home inventory
No information about pricing for homes in the Homes by HIP program or financing terms available
Mutual Self-Help program details are minimal—no clarity on construction timelines, participant requirements, or selection process
best_for: Very-low to low-income families in Central Florida seeking free, expert homeownership counseling without pressure
First-time homebuyers who need comprehensive education on the entire purchase process from credit to closing
Families interested in energy-efficient affordable homes in the five-county service area
Individuals wanting to participate in community building through mutual self-help housing construction
diagnosis: Homes In Partnership is ideal for low-to-moderate income Central Florida residents seeking free, non-profit housing counseling and wanting to build real equity through affordable homeownership. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to five Florida counties and the need to verify current program capacity and eligibility requirements before applying.
services: Fair-market affordable home sales through Homes by HIP program
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
